words.def: used when you are leaving somebody or when somebody else is leaving
words.example: Goodbye! It was great to meet you.

words.def: the father of your father or mother
words.example: The firm had been founded by his grandfather.

words.def: the mother of your father or mother
words.example: I always loved going to visit my grandmother.

words.def: the father or mother of your father or mother
words.example: The children are staying with their grandparents.

words.def: very good or pleasant
having the colour of grass or the leaves of most plants and trees
having the colour of smoke or ashes
a number of people or things that are together in the same place or that are connected in some way
words.example: group of somebody/something, a group of people/students/friends

words.def: to increase in size, number, strength or quality
words.example: The sector is growing at a phenomenal rate.

words.def: a musical instrument that usually has six strings and that you play with your fingers or with a plectrum
the substance that looks like a mass of fine threads growing on a person's head
to take place, especially without being planned
feeling or showing pleasure; pleased
difficult to do, understand or answer
a piece of clothing made to fit the head, often with a brim (= a flat edge that sticks out), usually worn out of doors
a male person or animal that has already been mentioned or is easily identified
words.example: Everyone liked my father—he was the perfect gentleman.

words.def: the condition of a person’s body or mind
words.example: to be good/bad for your health

words.def: having good health and not likely to become ill
to be aware of sounds with your ears
used as a greeting when you meet somebody, in an email, when you answer the phone or when you want to attract somebody’s attention
of or belonging to a woman or girl who has already been mentioned or is easily identified
used after a verb or preposition to mean ‘in, at or to this position or place’
used to say hello
used as the object of a verb, after the verb be or after a preposition to refer to a male person or animal that has already been mentioned or is easily identified
all the events that happened in the past
words.example: in history, The battle was a turning point in human history.

words.def: an activity that you do for pleasure when you are not working
a period of time when you are not at work or school
work that is given by teachers for students to do at home
words.example: I always do my homework on the bus.

words.def: a large animal with four legs, a mane (= long thick hair on its neck) and a tail. Horses are used for riding on, pulling carriages, etc.
a large building where people who are ill or injured are given medical treatment and care
words.example: to/into (the) hospital, (British English), He had to go to/into hospital for treatment.

words.def: having a high temperature; producing heat
a building where people stay, usually for a short time, paying for their rooms and meals
60 minutes; one of the 24 parts that a day is divided into
words.example: I spent an hour on the phone.

words.def: in what way or manner
feeling that you want to eat something
the man that somebody is married to; a married man
water that has frozen and become solid
words.example: There was ice on the windows.

words.def: a plan, thought or suggestion, especially about what to do in a particular situation
words.example: It would be a good idea to call before we leave.

words.def: used to say that one thing can, will or might happen or be true, depending on another thing happening or being true
words.example: If you see him, give him this note.

words.def: to form a picture in your mind of what something might be like
words.example: imagine something, The house was just as she had imagined it.

words.def: having a great effect on people or things; of great value
facts or details about somebody/something
giving your attention to something because you enjoy finding out about it or doing it; showing interest in something and finding it exciting
attracting your attention because it is/they are special, exciting or unusual
words.example: The article raises several interesting questions.

words.def: an international computer network connecting other networks and computers that allows people to share information around the world
a formal meeting at which somebody is asked questions to see if they are suitable for a particular job, or for a course of study at a college, university, etc.
to a position in or inside something
to tell two or more people who have not met before what each other’s names are; to tell somebody what your name is
words.example: introduce somebody, Allow me to introduce my mother.

words.def: a piece of land that is completely surrounded by water
belonging to or connected with a thing, an animal or a baby
words.example: Turn the box on its side.

words.def: a piece of clothing worn on the top half of the body over a shirt, etc. that has arms and fastens down the front; a short, light coat
the 1st month of the year, between December and February
trousers made of denim (= a type of strong cotton)
work for which you receive regular payment
words.example: I don't have a job at present.

words.def: to fix or connect two or more things together
words.example: join A to/onto B, Join one section of pipe to the next.

words.def: the liquid that comes from fruit or vegetables; a drink made from this
the 7th month of the year, between June and August
words.example: in July, She was born in July.

words.def: the 6th month of the year, between May and July
to continue to have something and not give it back or throw it away
a room in which meals are cooked or prepared
words.example: We ate at the kitchen table.

words.def: to have information in your mind as a result of experience or because you have learned or been told it
words.example: know something, No one knows the answer.

words.def: the system of communication in speech and writing that is used by people of a particular country or area
big in size or quantity
arriving, happening or done after the expected, arranged or usual time
to gain knowledge or skill by studying, from experience, from being taught, etc.
words.example: learn something, to learn a language/skill/trade

words.def: on the side of your body that is towards the west when you are facing north
words.example: Fewer people write with their left hand than with their right.

words.def: one of the long parts that connect the feet to the rest of the body
words.example: Sit on the floor, stretching your legs out in front of you.

words.def: a period of time in which somebody is taught something
used for making suggestions or as a polite way of telling people what you want them to do
a written, typed or printed message that is put in an envelope or attached to an email and sent to somebody
a building in which collections of books, newspapers, etc. and sometimes films and recorded music are kept for people to read, study or borrow
the ability to breathe, grow, produce young, etc. that people, animals and plants have before they die and that objects do not have
words.example: This could mean the difference between life and death.

words.def: a large, powerful animal of the cat family that hunts in groups and lives in parts of Africa and southern Asia. Lions have yellow-brown fur and the male has a mane (= long, thick hair round its neck).
words.example: The lion shook its mane and roared.

words.def: a series of names, items, figures, etc., especially when they are written or printed
words.example: The guest list includes numerous celebrities.

words.def: to pay attention to somebody/something that you can hear
words.example: Listen! What's that noise? Can you hear it?

words.def: measuring or covering a great length or distance, or a greater length or distance than usual
used with verbs to mean ‘a great amount’
words.example: I care a lot about you.

words.def: a very strong feeling of liking and caring for somebody/something, especially a member of your family or a friend
words.example: She has earned the love and respect of many people.

words.def: a meal eaten in the middle of the day
a piece of equipment with many parts that work together to do a particular task. The power used to work a machine may be electricity, steam, gas, etc. or human power.
words.example: Machines have replaced human labour in many industries.

words.def: a type of large thin book with a paper cover that you can buy every week or month, containing articles, photographs, etc., often on a particular topic; a similar collection of articles, etc. that appears regularly online
an adult male human

used with plural nouns and verbs, especially in negative sentences or in more formal English, to mean ‘a large number of’. Also used in questions to ask about the size of a number, and with ‘as’, ‘so’ and ‘too’.
words.example: We don't have very many copies left.

words.def: a sports event where people or teams compete against each other
used when you are not certain that something will happen or that something is true or is a correct number
words.example: Maybe he'll come, maybe he won't.

words.def: the form of I that is used when the speaker or writer is the object of a verb or preposition, or after the verb be
an occasion when people eat food, especially breakfast, lunch or dinner
words.example: Try not to eat between meals.

words.def: the soft part of an animal or a bird that can be eaten as food; a particular type of this
to be in the same place as somebody by chance and talk to them
words.example: Maybe we'll meet again some time.

words.def: an occasion when people come together to discuss or decide something
words.example: A hundred people attended the public meeting.

words.def: a written or spoken piece of information, etc. that you send to somebody or leave for somebody when you cannot speak to them yourself
words.example: There were no messages for me at the hotel.

words.def: the white liquid produced by cows, goats and some other animals as food for their young and used as a drink by humans
to be or arrive too late for something
words.example: If I don't leave now I'll miss my plane.

words.def: a very short period of time
words.example: Could you wait a moment, please?

words.def: the day of the week after Sunday and before Tuesday, the first day of the working week
what you earn by working or selling things, and use to buy things
any of the twelve periods of time into which the year is divided, for example May or June
words.example: The months of July and August are the hottest.

words.def: the early part of the day from the time when people wake up until 12 o'clock in the middle of the day or before lunch
words.example: They left for Spain early this morning.
